en.extract:
The coastal cisticola (Cisticola haematocephalus), also known as the umbabird, is a species of bird in the family Cisticolidae. It is found on the coastal plain of East Africa.
Alternate common names are Mombasa black-backed cisticola.


Taxonomy
The coastal cisticola is monotypic. This taxon was split from the winding cisticola by the IOC and HBW, as were the rufous-winged cisticola, Luapula cisticola and Ethiopian cisticola. The Clements (2017) and Howard and Moore (2014) world lists consider these taxa as a single species, the winding cisticola C. galactotes (sensu lato).


Distribution and habitat
This species is found on the coastal plain of East Africa between 5°N to 10°S (southern Somalia to north-eastern Tanzania).
Its natural habitats are tropical seasonally wet or flooded lowland grassland and swamps.


References


External links

 Data related to Cisticola haematocephalus at Wikispecies

The '''coastal cisticola''' ('''''Cisticola haematocephalus'''''), also known as the '''umbabird''', is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Cisticolidae]]. It is found on the coastal plain of [[East Africa]].<ref name="iucn status 20 November 2021" /><ref name="Ryan2017">{{cite web|last1=Ryan|first1=Peter|title=Coastal Cisticola (''Cisticola haematocephalus'')|url= http://www.hbw.com/node/58624|website=Handbook of the Birds of the World Alive|publisher=Lynx Edicions|access-date=21 August 2017|location=Barcelona|language=en}}</ref>

Alternate [[common name]]s are '''Mombasa black-backed cisticola'''.<ref name="AVIBASE">{{avibase|E88C0B71|Cisticola haematocephalus}}</ref>

==Taxonomy==
The coastal cisticola is monotypic.<ref name="Gill&Donsker2017">{{cite report|last1=Gill|first1=F.|last2=Donsker|first2=D.|title=IOC World Bird List (v 7.3)|date=2017|doi=10.14344/IOC.ML.7.3|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/|access-date=21 August 2017|doi-access=free}}</ref> This taxon was split from the [[winding cisticola]] by the [[Birds of the World: Recommended English Names|IOC]] and [[HBW and BirdLife International Illustrated Checklist of the Birds of the World|HBW]], as were the [[rufous-winged cisticola]], [[Luapula cisticola]] and [[Ethiopian cisticola]]. The [[The Clements Checklist of Birds of the World|Clements]] (2017) and [[Howard and Moore Complete Checklist of the Birds of the World|Howard and Moore]] (2014) world lists consider these taxa as a single species, the '''winding cisticola ''C. galactotes'' (sensu lato)'''.<ref name="AVIBASE"/>

==Distribution and habitat==
This species is found on the coastal plain of East Africa between 5°N to 10°S (southern Somalia to north-eastern Tanzania).<ref name="Ryan2017"/>

Its natural [[habitat]]s are tropical seasonally wet or flooded lowland [[grassland]] and [[swamp]]s.
